name: Cosmoverse 2025
attendance: Marketing pre-event materials cited 10,000+ attendees and 500+ speakers; post-event recaps describe 60+ curated sessions across ~10 tracks plus a 100-builder Hackmos x DoraHacks hackathon. Exact verified headcount not published in independent coverage.
defi_themes: Stablecoins and institutional stablecoin infrastructure (Visa pilot for a stablecoin liquidity and funding model with global banks)
CBDCs / Digital Euro and on-chain finance (ECB and Croatian National Bank; Cosmos Labs building two Latin American CBDCs)
Real-world asset (RWA) tokenization across banking, gaming, government, and asset management
Convergence of traditional finance (TradFi) and DeFi / institutional DeFi
Sovereign and neutral blockchain infrastructure as a base layer for regulated finance
Interoperability and cross-chain finance (IBC, EVM interoperability) for institutional users
Regulation and policy dialogue with central banks and securities commissions
ATOM and Cosmos Stack positioned for enterprise / Fortune 500 financial deployments

notable_panels.title: Digital Euro in the Age of On-Chain Finance
notable_panels.topic: CBDC / Digital Euro institutional design and 2029 implementation roadmap; intersection of central bank money and on-chain finance
notable_panels.speakers: Jurgen Schaaf (European Central Bank), Linardo Martincevic (Croatian National Bank)

key_takeaways: Cosmoverse 2025 marked a clear pivot toward institutional and regulated finance, branded around the convergence of TradFi, central banks, and sovereign Cosmos infrastructure rather than retail DeFi.

Central bank participation was the headline DeFi-adjacent story: the ECB and Croatian National Bank discussed a Digital Euro roadmap (targeting ~2029) in the 'Digital Euro in the Age of On-Chain Finance' session.
Stablecoins moved into the institutional spotlight, with Visa disclosing a pilot for a stablecoin liquidity and funding model with global banks.
Cosmos Labs confirmed it is building two CBDCs in Latin America, positioning the Cosmos Stack as government-grade financial infrastructure.
RWA / tokenization was a core track, framed around enterprise adoption (banking, asset management, government, gaming) rather than speculative yield.
Theme of 'sovereign' and neutral blockchain infrastructure dominated: Cosmos pitched IBC interoperability and app-chains as the settlement layer for regulated institutional finance.
At the closing ceremony, Cosmoverse 2026 was announced for Hong Kong (Nov 4-6, 2026), alongside Hong Kong FinTech Week, deepening the institutional/Asia finance focus.

Coverage was light on classic DeFi primitives (DEXs, lending, perps, restaking, on-chain treasuries); the DeFi narrative was overwhelmingly stablecoins, CBDCs, tokenization, and institutional integration.
